situation: User explicitly invoked the self-improving-agent skill in a frontend project that had no existing memory scaffold.
task: Establish a minimal, repo-local self-improvement structure and record the first session.
what_went_well: Repository was clean, so bootstrap changes were low risk
No existing self-improvement files meant there was no migration burden
Documentation-first placement fit the current project organization
what_went_wrong: The repository had no prior conventions for agent memory or hook integration
root_cause: Self-improvement capability was invoked before any project-local persistence model existed.
solution: Created docs/self-improvement with semantic, episodic, and working memory files plus a docs entry point.
lesson: For first adoption, prefer a visible documentation scaffold over hidden automation.
